tasks.title: Bootstrap the TypeScript workspace and zero-weight project skeleton
tasks.details: Create the Node.js 22+, strict TypeScript, ESM-only foundation with the minimal dependency policy and the directory layout described in the spec.
steps: Create package metadata, tsconfig, lint/format conventions, and dev scripts with pnpm and tsx.
Create the src/, skills/, and docs-facing root files expected by the architecture.
Document the repo conventions so future modules preserve the small-core boundary.

tasks.title: Define shared core types, schemas, IDs, and boundary validators
tasks.details: Implement the shared contracts for Task, Run, Session, Profile, Skill, Artifact, Hypothesis, approvals, and policy decisions with runtime validation only at system boundaries.
steps: Author TypeScript interfaces in shared types modules.
Add zod schemas for persisted state, provider I/O, and skill frontmatter boundaries.
Centralize ID generation, timestamps, and serialization helpers.

tasks.title: Implement file-backed storage for tasks, runs, sessions, artifacts, and experiment state
tasks.details: Build durable local persistence with clear separation between immutable trace/evidence and mutable runtime state.
steps: Define on-disk formats and directory structure for persisted objects.
Implement CRUD modules for tasks, runs, sessions, artifacts, and experiment bundles.
Add tests for atomic writes, reload behavior, and schema validation.

tasks.title: Build the browser provider contract and Steel provider implementation
tasks.details: Implement the provider abstraction around session lifecycle, observation, execution, and optional raw access, starting with Steel as the primary remote browser backend.
steps: Create the BrowserProvider interface and provider-specific input/output schemas.
Implement Steel session creation, lookup, shutdown, and connection plumbing.
Handle API key configuration and provider errors without leaking secrets into model context.

tasks.title: Implement browser observation bundles with compact summaries and artifacts
tasks.details: Ship browser.observe() so the runtime can inspect a real page cheaply before and after actions.
steps: Capture active tab, tab list, URL, title, focus context, and compact page summary.
Persist screenshot, HTML, and markdown artifacts and link them from the observation.
Normalize output for compare views and tests.

tasks.title: Implement code-first browser execution with helper loading and raw escape hatch
tasks.details: Ship browser.exec() and browser.raw() so the agent can act through inspectable code while preserving a lower-level escape hatch.
steps: Execute JS or TS snippets against the current session with timeout and target routing.
Support helper imports or attachments without growing a DSL.
Trace executed code, outputs, artifacts, and raw protocol usage explicitly.

tasks.title: Create the thin helper module system for forms, clicks, uploads, and tables
tasks.details: Add editable helper modules that reduce noise for common interactions but preserve direct code access and diffable changes.
steps: Implement minimal helpers for forms, clicks, uploads, and table extraction.
Define conventions for task-local edits and helper artifact diffs.
Add tests showing helper use and escape-hatch fallback.

tasks.title: Implement the skill file format, parser, matcher, and on-demand loader
tasks.details: Build durable file-based skills that capture reusable site knowledge without secrets or run diaries.
steps: Define skill frontmatter and section parsing for facts, selectors, routes, waits, and traps.
Match skills by hostname, tags, and explicit user hints.
Load only relevant skills into runtime context and trace every load.

tasks.title: Build the trace event pipeline and artifact registry
tasks.details: Implement immutable trace capture for every run so evidence is the product, not an afterthought.
steps: Persist typed trace events for observations, code execs, policy checks, approvals, skills, and errors.
Register and retrieve artifact metadata across screenshots, HTML, markdown, downloads, diffs, and notes.
Enforce the minimum trace policy from the spec.

tasks.title: Implement the policy engine, rules, and approval request flow
tasks.details: Keep policy outside the reasoning loop with explicit allow, deny, and require-approval decisions for destructive or privileged actions.
steps: Encode baseline approval-required actions and auth wall rules.
Implement policy checks for proposed actions and profile usage.
Persist approval requests and outcomes into run trace and storage.

tasks.title: Implement the LLM provider boundary and prompt/context assembly
tasks.details: Create a small model boundary that assembles task, skill, observation, and trace context without turning prompt context into the integration bus.
steps: Implement provider interfaces for OpenAI and placeholder portability hooks.
Build compact context assembly with task, skills, observations, and recent evidence.
Define the proposed action schema and model response validation.

tasks.title: Build the core agent runtime loop for task execution
tasks.details: Implement the minimal runtime that loads context, attaches a session, requests the next move, executes it, records evidence, and stops on the right conditions.
steps: Implement task ingestion, session attachment, initial observation, and loop orchestration.
Integrate policy checks, browser operations, trace persistence, and progress evaluation.
Stop on completion, denial, auth wall, cancellation, or budget exhaustion.

tasks.title: Implement run classification and final outcome summarization
tasks.details: Classify each run into task-complete, partial success, blocked auth, site error, agent error, infra error, counterexample, or ambiguous, with confidence and notes.
steps: Implement classification rules and summary generation from evidence.
Store final outcome summaries on runs and expose them to downstream compare views.
Test edge cases for ambiguous and failure-heavy runs.

tasks.title: Implement branching, hypothesis tracking, and experiment bundles
tasks.details: Make experiments first-class so the runtime can branch when uncertainty matters and record the rationale for each variant.
steps: Create hypothesis objects, branch metadata, and experiment bundle persistence.
Implement branch creation triggers and run linkage.
Support ablations, replications, and counterexample search as explicit run types.

tasks.title: Implement compare-view data generation across runs
tasks.details: Generate compare-ready summaries for run end state, step count, latency, artifacts, profile used, helper version, and hypothesis association.
steps: Define comparison specs and normalized dimensions.
Build compare data from traces, runs, artifacts, and sessions.
Test cross-branch comparisons and partial-data cases.

tasks.title: Implement skill proposal and promotion workflows
tasks.details: Convert reusable discoveries into reviewable skill patches without storing secrets, transcripts, or one-off narration.
steps: Detect reusable facts, selectors, routes, waits, and traps from completed runs.
Persist skill proposals as artifacts and diffs.
Add promotion flow support with review or policy gates.

tasks.title: Build the CLI surface for running tasks, investigations, and experiments
tasks.details: Provide a practical operator-facing entry point for creating tasks, selecting profiles, invoking runs, and reviewing outputs without framework overhead.
steps: Design a minimal command surface for task creation and run execution.
Support mode selection, profile/session options, and approval interaction.
Print concise summaries and artifact locations for completed runs.

tasks.title: Build the minimal run review interface for traces, artifacts, approvals, and comparisons
tasks.details: Expose the required observability features so users can inspect evidence, not just final prose.
steps: Design a lightweight local review surface or report renderer for timelines and artifacts.
Show latest screenshot, executed code, loaded skills, session/profile info, and approvals.
Add compare views for branched runs.

tasks.title: Implement profile selection, auth wall handling, and session safety controls
tasks.details: Keep identity separate from execution, stop at auth walls, and never silently escalate to privileged profiles.
steps: Add profile references, explicit selection rules, and session-profile linkage.
Handle missing credentials by requesting user assistance instead of improvising.
Test privileged-profile policy interactions and auth-failure flows.

tasks.title: Add evaluation harnesses and benchmark tasks for completion, learning, reliability, efficiency, and safety
tasks.details: Turn the spec’s evaluation strategy into repeatable measurements against realistic browser tasks and investigations.
steps: Define benchmark tasks and expected evidence outputs.
Collect metrics for success, rerun consistency, token/browser minute cost, and policy compliance.
Automate reports for regression tracking.

tasks.title: Build the full automated test suite across unit, integration, and end-to-end browser flows
tasks.details: Back the runtime with extensive tests so the core stays small, inspectable, and hard to accidentally braid together.
steps: Add unit coverage for schemas, storage, policy, skills, and classification.
Add integration coverage for provider, bridge, trace, and runtime orchestration.
Add end-to-end task, investigate, and experiment flow tests against controlled targets.

tasks.title: Write operator and developer documentation for the MVP system
tasks.details: Document how to configure providers, define skills, run tasks, inspect traces, handle approvals, and extend the system without adding framework weight.
steps: Write setup and usage docs for local development and Steel configuration.
Document skill authoring, promotion, and safety rules.
Document architecture boundaries and extension points for future maintainers.
